description: Hubble Ready CLI Testing Framework - Enable automated firmware testing through enhanced CLI commands

userStories.title: Add --address parameter to ready scan
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to filter scan results to a specific device address so that I can verify a specific device is advertising without interactive selection.
acceptanceCriteria: Command executes: `hubblenetwork ready scan --address AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF --format json`
Returns empty array if device not found (exit code 0)
Returns single-element array if device found
Case-insensitive address matching works (test AA:BB:CC vs aa:bb:cc)
Tabular output shows only filtered device when `--address` provided

userStories.title: Add --address parameter to ready info
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to connect directly to a device by address so that I can quickly inspect characteristics without interactive selection during development.
acceptanceCriteria: Command executes: `hubblenetwork ready info --address AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF --format json`
Connects directly without scanning when `--address` provided
Returns error with exit code 2 if connection fails
Existing scan+selection behavior unchanged when `--address` omitted
JSON output includes all characteristics on success

userStories.title: Add --timeout parameter to all ready commands
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I want to control connection timeouts for all ready commands so that I can optimize test suite execution time and handle flaky connections.
acceptanceCriteria: All existing ready commands support `--timeout` parameter
New commands added in future stories must support `--timeout`
Timeout errors return exit code 2 consistently
JSON error output includes timeout information

userStories.title: Add ATT error codes and BleError exception to errors.py
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want ATT error code constants and mappings defined in errors.py so that I can properly handle and report GATT operation failures in validation tests.
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 ATT error code constants defined
`ATT_ERROR_NAMES` dictionary maps all codes to human-readable names

userStories.title: Update JSON output structure to specification format
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want all ready commands to output JSON in a consistent, documented structure so that I can reliably parse test results and make decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: All top-level fields present: success, command, device, result/error, duration_ms
Error objects include code, name, message fields
BleError exceptions format correctly with ATT error codes
Device object includes address (required) and name (optional)
Duration tracking implemented (use time.monotonic() for timing)
JSON is valid and parseable (test with `jq` or `json.loads()`)

userStories.title: Add WriteResult, TestResult, ValidationResult dataclasses to ready.py
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want typed dataclasses for write results and test results so that the codebase maintains type safety and clear interfaces for testing functions.
acceptanceCriteria: All dataclasses have `to_dict()` methods for JSON serialization
Dataclasses are importable: `from hubblenetwork.ready import WriteResult, TestResult, ValidationResult`

userStories.title: Implement ready read-status command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to read just the Status characteristic so that I can verify version and provisioning flags during development without running full provisioning.
acceptanceCriteria: `read_status()` function in ready.py connects and reads Status characteristic
JSON output matches specification format with version and flags objects
Tabular output displays version, mode, and flags in human-readable format
BleError raised and formatted correctly on connection failure
Exit code 0 on success, 2 on error

userStories.title: Implement ready read-key-info command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to read the Device Key characteristic to determine encryption mode so that I can verify the device advertises the correct AES mode during testing.
acceptanceCriteria: `read_key_info()` function in ready.py reads Device Key characteristic
JSON output includes encryption_mode, encryption_mode_code, key_size_bytes
Tabular output displays encryption mode and key size
Exit code 0 on success, 2 on error

userStories.title: Implement ready read-config command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to read the Device Configuration characteristic so that I can verify EID type and pool size settings without running full provisioning.
acceptanceCriteria: `DeviceConfig` dataclass defined with eid_type, rotation_period, pool_size, raw_bytes
JSON output includes eid_type, eid_type_code, rotation_period_seconds, pool_size, raw_bytes
Correctly parses UTC mode (0x00) and Counter mode (0x01)
Tabular output displays configuration in human-readable format
Exit code 0 on success, 2 on error

userStories.title: Implement ready read-time command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to read the Epoch Time characteristic so that I can verify time synchronization without running full provisioning.
acceptanceCriteria: Function returns Unix timestamp as integer
JSON output includes timestamp and timestamp_iso (ISO 8601 format)
Tabular output displays timestamp in multiple formats
Exit code 0 on success, 2 on error

userStories.title: Implement ready write-key command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to write an encryption key to the Device Key characteristic so that I can test key write handling in isolation from full provisioning.
acceptanceCriteria: `write_key()` function writes encryption key to device
Function automatically reads encryption mode first to validate key length
JSON output includes key_written flag and key_size_bytes on success
JSON output includes ATT error code and message on failure
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on write failure (wrong length), 2 on connection error

userStories.title: Implement ready write-config command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to write device configuration (EID type, pool size) so that I can test configuration validation and write handling in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Function validates EID type, pool size, rotation period locally
JSON output includes config_written, eid_type, pool_size, rotation_period
Validation errors return appropriate ATT error codes (0x85, 0x86)
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on validation failure, 2 on connection error

userStories.title: Implement ready write-time command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to write epoch time to the device so that I can test time write handling and verify time synchronization in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: `write_time()` function writes epoch time to device
Function uses current time if timestamp not provided
JSON output includes time_written, timestamp, timestamp_iso
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on write failure, 2 on connection error
